source_paper_title: Riemannian Anderson Mixing Methods for Minimizing C2 Functions on Riemannian Manifolds
context:
Page 10, Section 4 (Regularied RAM and Global Convergence Analysis); also reiterated on page 14, Section 5.1 (Details Concerning Numerical Implementations)

The global convergence of Anderson mixing remains an problem, even in the Euclidean setting.
title: Global convergence of (unregularized) Anderson mixing for nonlinear fixed-point iterations
background_latex: Let \(n\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(g:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^n\) be a continuously differentiable map and consider the fixed-point problem of finding \(x^\star\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that \(g(x^\star)=x^\star\). Define the residual \(r(x):=g(x)-x\). Fix a memory parameter \(m\in\mathbb{N}\) and a mixing parameter \(\beta_k>0\). Given iterates \(\{x_k\}_{k\ge 0}\), define \(r_k:=r(x_k)\), forward differences \(\Delta x_j:=x_{j+1}-x_j\) and \(\Delta r_j:=r_{j+1}-r_j\). For \(m_k:=\min\{m,k\}\), define matrices \(X_k:=[\Delta x_{k-m_k},\ldots,\Delta x_{k-1}]\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m_k}\) and \(R_k:=[\Delta r_{k-m_k},\ldots,\Delta r_{k-1}]\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m_k}\). Let \(\Gamma_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\) be any minimizer of the least-squares problem \(\Gamma_k\in\arg\min_{\Gamma\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}}\|r_k-R_k\Gamma\|_2\). The (unregularized) Anderson mixing update is \[x_{k+1}=x_k+\beta_k r_k-(X_k+\beta_k R_k)\Gamma_k.\]
problem_definition_latex: Prove (or disprove by counterexample) global convergence of the unregularized Anderson mixing iteration to a fixed point \(x^\star\) under verifiable assumptions on \(g\) and parameters \(\{\beta_k\}\); that is, identify conditions ensuring that for every initial point \(x_0\in\mathbb{R}^n\), the sequence \(\{x_k\}\) generated by the update \(x_{k+1}=x_k+\beta_k r_k-(X_k+\beta_k R_k)\Gamma_k\) is well-defined for all \(k\) and satisfies \(\lim_{k\to\infty}\|r(x_k)\|_2=0\) (equivalently, every limit point is a fixed point of \(g\), and ideally \(x_k\to x^\star\)).
latex:
Let \(n\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(g:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^n\) be a continuously differentiable map and consider the fixed-point problem of finding \(x^\star\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that \(g(x^\star)=x^\star\). Define the residual \(r(x):=g(x)-x\). Fix a memory parameter \(m\in\mathbb{N}\) and a mixing parameter \(\beta_k>0\). Given iterates \(\{x_k\}_{k\ge 0}\), define \(r_k:=r(x_k)\), forward differences \(\Delta x_j:=x_{j+1}-x_j\) and \(\Delta r_j:=r_{j+1}-r_j\). For \(m_k:=\min\{m,k\}\), define matrices \(X_k:=[\Delta x_{k-m_k},\ldots,\Delta x_{k-1}]\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m_k}\) and \(R_k:=[\Delta r_{k-m_k},\ldots,\Delta r_{k-1}]\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m_k}\). Let \(\Gamma_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\) be any minimizer of the least-squares problem \(\Gamma_k\in\arg\min_{\Gamma\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}}\|r_k-R_k\Gamma\|_2\). The (unregularized) Anderson mixing update is \[x_{k+1}=x_k+\beta_k r_k-(X_k+\beta_k R_k)\Gamma_k.\]

Prove (or disprove by counterexample) global convergence of the unregularized Anderson mixing iteration to a fixed point \(x^\star\) under verifiable assumptions on \(g\) and parameters \(\{\beta_k\}\); that is, identify conditions ensuring that for every initial point \(x_0\in\mathbb{R}^n\), the sequence \(\{x_k\}\) generated by the update \(x_{k+1}=x_k+\beta_k r_k-(X_k+\beta_k R_k)\Gamma_k\) is well-defined for all \(k\) and satisfies \(\lim_{k\to\infty}\|r(x_k)\|_2=0\) (equivalently, every limit point is a fixed point of \(g\), and ideally \(x_k\to x^\star\)).
